Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process event log from a fictional financial services company It contains three cases C001 C002 C003 Each case has events with activities Initiate Request Verify Documents repeated in same case and Call Customer repeated in some cases Approve Request appears once per case always at the end There is also Adjust Account activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task Identify activities that appear multiple times within the same case and interpret how their meaning changes depending on context We need to rename them with disambiguation so that it becomes clear what role they play in process flow Then present a revised event log with new names and then justify naming choices briefly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C001 we see two instances of Verify Documents where one instance is used as initial check on submitted documents and the other after additional docs have been uploaded

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The first Verify Documents seems to be performed when documents are submitted via portal but since it is incomplete maybe not valid Actually check context for first Verify Documents agent checks identity documents then a call is made because verification is incomplete then customer uploads additional document and then re checking of documents takes place

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can rename these two Verify Documents events as something like Initial Document Verification vs Reverification after Additional Submission

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C002 two events have same name Call Customer The first call is made to clarify an account discrepancy The second call is a follow up call to confirm adjustments that were made

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we can rename them as Initial Call for Account Discrepancy vs Follow Up Confirmation Call

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C003 again two Verify Documents events appear first initial physical verification second post adjustment re check of documents

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the recurring activities are Verify Documents recurs multiple times in cases C001 and C003 They have different roles initial verification vs re verification after submission of additional documents or post account adjustment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Call Customer events are not all identical in purpose and need distinct names based on context

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For C001 call event I might name it Outbound Call for Missing Documents For C002 first call Clarification Call for Account Discrepancy second call Follow up Confirmation Call For C003 call event Reminder Call to Return Customer

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Initiate Request appears once per case and does not need renaming Adjust Account appears only once per case where it is applicable Approve Request only occurs once so can be left unchanged

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I want to keep naming consistent Perhaps we can use a naming scheme like Initial Document Verification and Reverification of Documents consistently

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For call events use prefix based on type of call Missing Documents Clarify Account Discrepancy Confirm Adjustments Request Customer Return

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The additional context and resource columns support these distinctions because they show whether the step is initial follow up branch based online based or triggered by missing information

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus revised event log should keep unique activities unchanged and rename repeated activities according to sequence position and purpose
